prestazioni: Engine swap to a fully rebuilt and balanced Lancia V4 Tipo 818.303 (1.6L, 115 PS) with forged Mahle pistons (80.5mm bore), Carrillo connecting rods, and a billet crankshaft from JP Pistons, paired with a custom camshaft profile by Kent Cams (280° duration, 11.5mm lift) for high-RPM power delivery (8,500 RPM redline).

Installation of a custom-fabricated 4-into-1 stainless steel exhaust manifold by Janspeed Engineering, terminating in a 2.5-inch dual-exit system with a MagnaFlow 14815 muffler for optimized scavenging and a deep, aggressive tone without drone.

Upgraded ignition system featuring a Pertronix Ignitor III electronic ignition conversion, NGK B9EGV spark plugs gapped at 0.6mm, and a custom-mapped 123Tune+ standalone ECU (with Lancia Fulvia-specific firmware) for precise timing control and fuel delivery via Weber 45 DCOE carburetors (jetted with 160 main jets, 185 air correctors, and 45F9 emulsion tubes).
estetica: Full restoration with a custom body-color-matched paint scheme in Lancia Verde Muschio (original 1967 factory code 2.5G 2020) applied by Bare Metal Specialists, including hand-brushed aluminum lower body panels (replicating the 1967 HF Stradale aesthetic) and a clear-coated satin finish.

OMP Classic 1960s-style fiberglass hardtop with a custom rear spoiler (modeled after the 1968 Fulvia Sport Zagato) in carbon fiber, fabricated by Zender, with integrated LED brake light and a 3M Scotchcal matte black vinyl stripe running from the hood to the trunk lid.

Interior overhaul featuring Recaro SRD bucket seats (retrimmed in Nappa leather with Alcantara centers, color-matched to Lancia Rosso Bordeaux), a Momo Prototipo steering wheel (380mm diameter, suede grip), and a custom aluminum dashboard overlay by Speedhut with vintage-style Smiths gauges (3.5-inch tachometer, oil pressure, and water temperature).
